data.content:
### POST /auth/login Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/nextjs.mdx Authenticates a user with email and password credentials and redirects upon successful login. ```APIDOC ## POST /auth/login ### Description Authenticates a user with email and password credentials and redirects upon successful login. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /auth/login ### Parameters #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Required - User's email address for login - **password** (string) - Required - User's password for authentication ### Request Example { "email": "user@example.com", "password": "securePassword123" } ### Response #### Success Response (301) - Redirects to the specified origin URL #### Response Example HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently Location: https://example.com ``` -------------------------------- ### React TypeScript Component for Authenticated Route Redirection Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/ui-library/content/docs/react/password-based-auth.mdx This React functional component uses the `useEffect` hook to implement a client-side authentication check. It initializes a Supabase client and attempts to retrieve the current user's session. If the `getUser()` call returns an error (indicating no authenticated user), the component redirects the user to the `/login` route, ensuring only authenticated users can access the page. ```ts import { useEffect } from "react" import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js" export default function AuthenticatedRoute() { useEffect(() => { const checkAuth = async () => { const client = createClient() const { error } = await client.auth.getUser() if (error) { location.href = "/login" } } checkAuth() }, []) return
Authenticated page
; } ``` -------------------------------- ### Supabase Authenticated User JWT Token Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/jwt-fields.mdx Example JWT token structure for authenticated users with metadata and authentication method claims ```json { "aal": "aal1", "amr": [ { "method": "password", "timestamp": 1640991600 } ], "app_metadata": { "provider": "email", "providers": ["email"] }, "aud": "authenticated", "email": "user@example.com", "exp": 1640995200, "iat": 1640991600, "iss": "https://abcdefghijklmnopqrst.supabase.co/auth/v1", "phone": "", "role": "authenticated", "session_id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "sub": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "user_metadata": { "name": "John Doe" }, "is_anonymous": false } ``` -------------------------------- ### POST /auth/sign-up Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/nextjs.mdx Creates a new user account with email and password, then redirects to the callback URL for email verification. ```APIDOC ## POST /auth/sign-up ### Description Creates a new user account with email and password, then redirects to the callback URL for email verification. ### Method POST ### Endpoint /auth/sign-up ### Parameters #### Request Body - **email** (string) - Required - User's email address for registration - **password** (string) - Required - User's password for the account - **emailRedirectTo** (string) - Optional - URL to redirect after email verification ### Request Example { "email": "user@example.com", "password": "securePassword123", "options": { "emailRedirectTo": "https://example.com/auth/callback" } } ### Response #### Success Response (301) - Redirects to the specified origin URL #### Response Example HTTP/1.1 301 Moved Permanently Location: https://example.com ``` -------------------------------- ### Install and Configure Supabase Authentication in RedwoodJS Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/getting-started/tutorials/with-redwoodjs.mdx This command integrates Supabase authentication into the RedwoodJS project. It installs the necessary 'supabase-js' dependency, generates authentication-related files, and configures the Supabase client and hooks for authentication. ```bash yarn redwood setup auth supabase ```
